questions.question: What are public facilities?

options.A: Services provided for the benefit of all people

questions.explanation: Public facilities are services provided for the welfare of all citizens.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a public facility?
questions.explanation: Clean drinking water is an essential public facility.

questions.question: Who is mainly responsible for providing public facilities?
questions.explanation: The government is responsible for providing public facilities.

questions.question: Which facility is essential for maintaining health?
questions.explanation: Clean drinking water is necessary for good health.

questions.question: Which of the following is a public service?
questions.explanation: Public hospitals provide healthcare services to citizens.

questions.question: Why are public facilities important?

options.A: They improve people's quality of life

questions.explanation: Public facilities help people live healthy and comfortable lives.

questions.question: Which public facility helps people travel easily?
questions.explanation: Public transport helps people travel at low cost.

questions.question: Which public facility provides education to children?
questions.explanation: Government schools provide education as a public service.

questions.question: Which of the following is necessary for sanitation?
questions.explanation: Clean water is essential for sanitation and hygiene.

questions.question: Which public facility helps maintain law and order?
questions.explanation: Police services maintain safety and law enforcement.

questions.question: Which resource is discussed in the chapter as an important public facility?
questions.explanation: Water is one of the most important public facilities.

questions.question: Which organisation provides water supply in cities?
questions.explanation: Municipal corporations manage water supply in many cities.

questions.question: Which public facility helps people stay healthy?
questions.explanation: Public hospitals provide healthcare services.

questions.question: Which facility helps keep cities clean?
questions.explanation: Sanitation services maintain cleanliness and hygiene.

questions.question: Why should public facilities be available to everyone?
questions.explanation: Public facilities must be accessible to all citizens.

questions.question: Which of the following is a responsibility of the government?
questions.explanation: Governments provide essential services to citizens.

questions.question: Which facility helps in safe transportation?
questions.explanation: Public transport helps people travel safely and affordably.

questions.question: Which service provides electricity to homes?
questions.explanation: Electricity supply is a basic public service.

questions.question: Which public facility supports communication?
questions.explanation: Postal services help people send messages and parcels.

questions.question: Which public service ensures safety in society?
questions.explanation: Police protect citizens and maintain law and order.

questions.question: Which public facility helps in learning and education?
questions.explanation: Government schools provide education to children.

questions.question: Which resource is essential for cooking and drinking?
questions.explanation: Water is necessary for cooking and drinking.

questions.question: Which service provides medical care to citizens?
questions.explanation: Public hospitals provide healthcare services.

questions.question: Which facility helps maintain hygiene in society?
questions.explanation: Sanitation services keep the environment clean.

questions.question: Why are public facilities important in a country?

options.A: They support the well-being of citizens

questions.explanation: Public facilities improve the quality of life for citizens.
